---
title: "Coffee Shop Pro Forma"
subtitle: "A pyproforma walkthrough"
format: html
execute:
  echo: true
---

pyproforma is a code-first alternative to Excel for building financial models. Instead of
formula cells in a spreadsheet, you define line items as class attributes on a Python class.
You get version control, testing, and clean exports to tables and charts — with the same
structure any analyst would recognise.

This walkthrough builds a three-year income statement for a fictional coffee shop and
covers the core concepts along the way.

## Setup

```{python}
from IPython.display import HTML
from pyproforma import FixedLine, Format, FormulaLine, ProformaModel, ScalarLine
```

## Defining the model

Subclass `ProformaModel` and declare each line item as a class attribute. Setting
`default_periods` means you can instantiate without passing `periods=` every time.

```{python}
class CoffeeShop(ProformaModel):
    default_periods = [2024, 2025, 2026]
    period_label = "Year"

    # Revenue — hardcoded actuals / projections
    coffee_sales = FixedLine(
        values={2024: 280_000, 2025: 308_000, 2026: 339_000},
        label="Coffee Sales",
        tags=["revenue"],
        value_format=Format.CURRENCY_NO_DECIMALS,
    )
    food_sales = FixedLine(
        values={2024: 95_000, 2025: 105_000, 2026: 116_000},
        label="Food Sales",
        tags=["revenue"],
        value_format=Format.CURRENCY_NO_DECIMALS,
    )
    merchandise_sales = FixedLine(
        values={2024: 25_000, 2025: 27_000, 2026: 30_000},
        label="Merchandise",
        tags=["revenue"],
        value_format=Format.CURRENCY_NO_DECIMALS,
    )

    # COGS rates — single values shared across all periods
    coffee_cogs_rate = ScalarLine(
        value=0.35, label="Coffee COGS Rate", value_format=Format.PERCENT_ONE_DECIMAL
    )
    food_cogs_rate = ScalarLine(
        value=0.45, label="Food COGS Rate", value_format=Format.PERCENT_ONE_DECIMAL
    )

    # COGS — calculated from the rates above
    coffee_cogs = FormulaLine(
        formula=lambda li, t: li.coffee_sales[t] * li.coffee_cogs_rate,
        label="Coffee COGS",
        tags=["cogs"],
        value_format=Format.CURRENCY_NO_DECIMALS,
    )
    food_cogs = FormulaLine(
        formula=lambda li, t: li.food_sales[t] * li.food_cogs_rate,
        label="Food COGS",
        tags=["cogs"],
        value_format=Format.CURRENCY_NO_DECIMALS,
    )

    # Subtotals — tag sums roll up all items with a given tag
    total_revenue = FormulaLine(
        formula=lambda li, t: li.tag["revenue"][t],
        label="Total Revenue",
        value_format=Format.CURRENCY_NO_DECIMALS,
    )
    total_cogs = FormulaLine(
        formula=lambda li, t: li.tag["cogs"][t],
        label="Total COGS",
        value_format=Format.CURRENCY_NO_DECIMALS,
    )
    gross_profit = FormulaLine(
        formula=lambda li, t: li.total_revenue[t] - li.total_cogs[t],
        label="Gross Profit",
        value_format=Format.CURRENCY_NO_DECIMALS,
    )
    gross_margin = FormulaLine(
        formula=lambda li, t: li.gross_profit[t] / li.total_revenue[t],
        label="Gross Margin",
        value_format=Format.PERCENT_ONE_DECIMAL,
    )

    # Operating expenses
    rent = FixedLine(
        values={2024: 48_000, 2025: 49_440, 2026: 50_923},
        label="Rent",
        tags=["opex"],
        value_format=Format.CURRENCY_NO_DECIMALS,
    )
    labor = FixedLine(
        values={2024: 110_000, 2025: 118_000, 2026: 127_000},
        label="Labor",
        tags=["opex"],
        value_format=Format.CURRENCY_NO_DECIMALS,
    )
    utilities = FixedLine(
        values={2024: 12_000, 2025: 12_600, 2026: 13_230},
        label="Utilities",
        tags=["opex"],
        value_format=Format.CURRENCY_NO_DECIMALS,
    )
    marketing = FixedLine(
        values={2024: 15_000, 2025: 17_000, 2026: 19_000},
        label="Marketing",
        tags=["opex"],
        value_format=Format.CURRENCY_NO_DECIMALS,
    )
    total_opex = FormulaLine(
        formula=lambda li, t: li.tag["opex"][t],
        label="Total Operating Expenses",
        value_format=Format.CURRENCY_NO_DECIMALS,
    )

    # Bottom line
    operating_profit = FormulaLine(
        formula=lambda li, t: li.gross_profit[t] - li.total_opex[t],
        label="Operating Profit",
        value_format=Format.CURRENCY_NO_DECIMALS,
    )
    operating_margin = FormulaLine(
        formula=lambda li, t: li.operating_profit[t] / li.total_revenue[t],
        label="Operating Margin",
        value_format=Format.PERCENT_ONE_DECIMAL,
    )
```

## Instantiating

Calling the class runs the calculation engine and resolves every formula across every period.
Because `default_periods` is set on the class, no arguments are required.

```{python}
model = CoffeeShop()
model
```

## Accessing values

Use dot notation to read any line item. Bracket notation works the same way and is useful
when the name is in a variable.

```{python}
model.total_revenue[2024]
```

```{python}
model.gross_margin[2026]
```

```{python}
# ScalarLine — not period-indexed
model.coffee_cogs_rate.value
```

## Line item types

There are three line item types used in this model:

| Type | Use | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `FixedLine` | Hardcoded values per period | `coffee_sales` |
| `ScalarLine` | A single value shared across all periods | `coffee_cogs_rate` |
| `FormulaLine` | Calculated from other items | `gross_profit` |

Formula lambdas receive `li` (a namespace for all items) and `t` (the current period).
Access period-indexed items with `li.name[t]` and scalars with `li.name`.

## Revenue

```{python}
from pyproforma.tables.row_types import BlankRow, HeaderRow, ItemRow, LabelRow, LineItemsTotalRow

revenue_template = [
    HeaderRow(),
    LabelRow("Revenue"),
    ItemRow("coffee_sales"),
    ItemRow("food_sales"),
    ItemRow("merchandise_sales"),
    LineItemsTotalRow(
        line_item_names=["coffee_sales", "food_sales", "merchandise_sales"],
        label="Total Revenue",
        bold=True,
    ),
]

HTML(model.tables.from_template(revenue_template).to_html())
```

## Cost of goods sold

COGS lines reference the scalar rates via `li.coffee_cogs_rate` — no `[t]` subscript needed
because scalars are not period-indexed.

```{python}
cogs_template = [
    HeaderRow(),
    LabelRow("Cost of Goods Sold"),
    ItemRow("coffee_cogs"),
    ItemRow("food_cogs"),
    LineItemsTotalRow(
        line_item_names=["coffee_cogs", "food_cogs"],
        label="Total COGS",
        bold=True,
    ),
]

HTML(model.tables.from_template(cogs_template).to_html())
```

## Tags

Tagging line items (`tags=["revenue"]`) lets formulas sum an entire category without
naming each item explicitly:

```python
total_revenue = FormulaLine(formula=lambda li, t: li.tag["revenue"][t], ...)
```

This means adding a new revenue stream only requires declaring it with `tags=["revenue"]` —
the total updates automatically.

## Income statement

```{python}
income_statement = [
    HeaderRow(),
    LabelRow("Revenue"),
    ItemRow("coffee_sales"),
    ItemRow("food_sales"),
    ItemRow("merchandise_sales"),
    ItemRow("total_revenue", bold=True, borders="top"),
    BlankRow(),
    LabelRow("Cost of Goods Sold"),
    ItemRow("coffee_cogs"),
    ItemRow("food_cogs"),
    ItemRow("total_cogs", bold=True, borders="top"),
    ItemRow("gross_profit", bold=True),
    ItemRow("gross_margin"),
    BlankRow(),
    LabelRow("Operating Expenses"),
    ItemRow("rent"),
    ItemRow("labor"),
    ItemRow("utilities"),
    ItemRow("marketing"),
    ItemRow("total_opex", bold=True, borders="top"),
    BlankRow(),
    ItemRow("operating_profit", bold=True, borders="top"),
    ItemRow("operating_margin"),
]

HTML(model.tables.from_template(income_statement).to_html())
```

## Charts

```{python}
model.charts.line_items(
    ["total_revenue", "gross_profit", "operating_profit"],
    chart_type="bar",
    title="Revenue & Profit",
).figure();
```

```{python}
model.charts.line_items(
    ["gross_margin", "operating_margin"],
    chart_type="line",
    title="Margin Trends",
).figure();
```

## Exports

Any table can be exported to Excel or converted to a pandas DataFrame:

```python
model.tables.from_template(income_statement).to_excel("coffee_shop.xlsx")
model.tables.from_template(income_statement).to_dataframe()
```
